name: Bhai Malkeet Singh (Chandigarh)
body: The information below was written by Veer Amanbir Singh, who learned from Bhai Malkeet Singh Ji for a short but memorable period of time.

Bhai Malkeet Singh Ji was born on March 5 , 1948, in the house of Bhai Darshan Singh Ji Dilruba. Naturally with a father like his, Bhai Sahib had an interest in Kirtan from a very young age. Bhai Sahib Ji was a natural talent at Kirtan. With such a beautiful voice at a young age, he was invited to do Kirtan at Alwar Samagam in 1974.

Bhai Sahib Ji also played the tabla very well. Sometime around the 1960s he went to Patna Sahib for work. While he was there, he was asked to play tabla with the famed jatha of Bhai Joginder Singh & Gurbachan Singh Ji of Patna Sahib. They were so amazed with how he played that they asked him to play with them for as long as he was there.

Not only was he very skilled with the Vaja and Tabla but he also played the Dilruba very well. As his father was getting older and Bhai Sahib’s skills with the Dilruba were growing, his father one day called him and gave him his Dilruba that he (his father) had hand-crafted himself. His father then said, ‘Ja Sangat Nu Nihal Kar, Hun Tu Seva Niba.’
Bhai Sahib Ji played this same Dilruba for his whole life and despite how old this Dilruba is, it has never broken out of ‘sur’ and always sounded melodious.

Another story I remember is when he was invited to do Kirtan at a University Program where Bhai Balbir Singh Ji was also to do Kirtan. After Bhai Balbir Singh Ji had finished doing Kirtan, Bhai Sahib was called to do Kirtan next. Bhai Balbir Singh Ji was in hurry as he had to go home, but when Bhai Sahib Ji first started doing Kirtan, Bhai Balbir Singh Ji came back from outside and watched in amazement as Bhai Sahib performed. Bhai Balbir Singh Ji then told someone to send a message to Bhai Sahib that he must come to Bhai Balbir Singh Ji’s house to eat.

Bhai Sahib Ji continued doing Kirtan at various Samagams. He was originally from Delhi, but he moved to Chandigarh in mid 1980s. In the early 2000s he was invited various times to Toronto, Canada for Kirtan. Later, in 2011,2012, and 2013, he did kirtan at Windsor, Ontario. On his last tour, he went to Surrey in 2014 and 2015. He left his mark on many students who had the great fortune to learn from him.

Bhai Sahib Ji spent his last few years passing on the Kirtan ‘Shaili’ that is so sparse today. He grew very ill for a few months and wasn’t able to teach or do kirtan. On the fateful morning of December 24, 2017, while listening to Asa Di Vaar as it ended and Ardaas began Bhai Sahib ji passed away peacefully.
If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Malkeet Singh Ji or his father Bhai Darshan Singh Dilruba Ji (Veera Ji), please contact us.
